hypothesis.statement: Around scheduled CPI releases, we test whether forward intraday behavior conditioned on the post-release window (and, as an offline-only split, on surprise magnitude) shows a measurable reversion-versus-continuation pattern relative to non-event sessions. Falsifiable conditioning hypothesis; not a profitability claim.
hypothesis.economic_rationale: Scheduled inflation-print information arrival can trigger an initial impulse followed by liquidity-provision dynamics; whether the post-impulse path reverts or continues is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve.

new.name: minutes_since_scheduled_event
new.reason: Elapsed-time-since-scheduled-release is a known-ahead clock relative to a known-ahead scheduled time; needs a new FeatureRequest extending SESSION_CALENDAR_ROLL with the event-schedule metadata surface. Scheduled time is known ahead; the clock derived from it is therefore point-in-time legal.

new.name: event_window_forward_return
new.reason: Optional: an event-window-anchored variant of the existing fixed_horizon forward-return label, restricting the anchor to in-window bars. Only needed if the study wants outcomes strictly anchored inside the CPI window rather than reusing the unrestricted fixed_horizon label with a window FEATURE filter. Prefer reuse over a new LabelSpec unless the window-anchoring is load-bearing.

lookahead_risk.description: CPI SURPRISE (actual vs consensus) is the natural conditioner but is knowable ONLY after the release. Using it as a live feature would be lookahead. Scheduled release time is known ahead and is safe.
lookahead_risk.mitigation: Scheduled release time carried as known-ahead point-in-time metadata (_require_metadata_known_as_of guard). Surprise magnitude is used only as an OFFLINE diagnostic split / labels-only conditioning, never wired into a live (causal) feature contract.

expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.statement: Conditioning on a scheduled-release clock is structurally distinct from realized-price-action and BBO-state conditioning used by the rejected mechanisms. The reversion-vs-continuation OUTCOME may overlap with vwap_session/sweep dynamics, so the orthogonality claim is weaker than for fomc_drift and must be checked, not assumed.
shared_inputs_with_dead: forward-return outcome labels are shared substrate (not a dead-mechanism input, but the reversion outcome is adjacent to vwap_session)
expected_orthogonality_to_dead_mechanism.diagnostic_check: Residualize forward outcomes against the vwap_session and regime dead-mechanism factors before scoring the CPI-window conditioner; report the conditioner's marginal contribution and an explicit overlap diagnostic.

capacity_turnover_note: Approximately 12 scheduled CPI releases per year; low occurrence count, N_eff-limited. Pool across instruments per Track-B pooled minimum. No capacity/turnover dollar claim.

data_dependency.detail: CPI scheduled release dates/times are public and small to encode; CPI surprise/actual values require an external economic-data feed. No economic-calendar source exists in the repo. External macro feed deferred.

value_free_status.claims: hypothesis pre-registration only; no alpha/tradability/profitability claim
